mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 4.0% threshold, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements and corporate margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during mid-cycle expansions, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero (t-stat < 1.5) when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and short-term equity returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches the 310.0 level, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 310.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of tightening periods with falling INDPRO, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS growth exceeds 0.25% per month.
